content.thinking:
The improvement is marginal (~0.3 tok/s). Let me consider keeping it - it's a code quality improvement (fewer ObjC calls, cleaner batching pattern) with a slight, consistent improvement. The code is cleaner and the results are consistently above baseline (223.6, 223.9, 223.6 vs 223.3).

Let me keep this and move on to a more impactful optimization in the next experiment.

content.text: The improvement is small but consistent: 223.6, 223.9, 223.6 vs 223.3 baseline. The code is cleaner (1 ObjC call instead of 3 for each Q4 buffer binding). Let me keep this.

input.summary: Batched setQ4Buffer from 3 individual ObjC message sends into a single setBuffers:offsets:withRange: call. The same MTLBuffer is bound at 3 consecutive indices (nibbles, scales, biases) with different offsets. Saves ~400 ObjC calls per decode step (7 Q4 bindings per block × 28 blocks × 2 saved calls each). Result: small but consistent improvement from 223.3 to ~223.7 tok/s (0.2%). P99 improved from 4711 to ~4660μs.
